title: Mapping ACS uncertainty with tidycensus
text: estimate population age 65 older summing age--sex cells table B01001. ACS API returns row per variable per tract, estimate margin error row.
code: library(acsmoe) library(tidycensus) library(dplyr) library(sf) library(ggplot2)  tidycensus::census_api_key(Sys.getenv("CENSUS_API_KEY"), install = FALSE) vars_65plus <- paste0("B01001_", sprintf("%03d", c(20:25, 44:49)))  ramsey_age <- get_acs(   geography = "tract",   variables = vars_65plus,   state = "MN",   county = "Ramsey",   year = 2022,   survey = "acs5",   geometry = TRUE )

title: Mapping ACS uncertainty with tidycensus
text: covariance supplied, acs_sum() matches standard Census root-sum-square MOE approximation used tidycensus::moe_sum(). benefit using acsmoe code path can later accept covariance .
code: ramsey_65plus <- ramsey_age |>   group_by(GEOID, NAME) |>   summarize(     estimate_65plus = sum(estimate),     moe_65plus = acs_sum(estimate, moe)$moe,     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   mutate(     cv_65plus = acs_cv(estimate_65plus, moe_65plus),     relative_moe = moe_65plus / estimate_65plus,     reliability = acs_reliability(estimate_65plus, moe_65plus)   )  ramsey_65plus |>   st_drop_geometry() |>   select(GEOID, estimate_65plus, moe_65plus, cv_65plus, reliability) |>   head()

title: Mapping ACS uncertainty with tidycensus
text: estimate map useful, incomplete . relative-MOE reliability maps show tract estimates less stable.
code: ggplot(ramsey_65plus) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = estimate_65plus), color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_fill_gradient(     low = "#f4f1de",     high = "#245953",     labels = scales::comma,     name = "Age 65+"   ) +   labs(     title = "Estimated population age 65+",     subtitle = "Ramsey County, MN tracts; ACS 2018-2022 5-year"   ) +   theme_void() ggplot(ramsey_65plus) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = relative_moe), color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_fill_gradient(     low = "#e8f3f1",     high = "#a23e48",     labels = scales::percent,     name = "MOE / estimate"   ) +   labs(     title = "Relative uncertainty in age 65+ estimates",     subtitle = "Higher values indicate less stable tract estimates"   ) +   theme_void() ggplot(ramsey_65plus) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = reliability), color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c(       reliable = "#2a9d8f",       caveat = "#e9c46a",       unreliable = "#e76f51"     ),     drop = FALSE,     name = "CV class"   ) +   labs(     title = "Reliability classes from coefficient of variation",     subtitle = "Default thresholds: reliable < 0.12, caveat < 0.40"   ) +   theme_void()

title: Mapping ACS uncertainty with tidycensus
text: Users often aggregate tracts service areas, planning districts, hand-built regions. create simple west/east split tract centroids demonstrate mechanics.
code: centroids <- st_coordinates(st_centroid(ramsey_65plus))  ramsey_regions <- ramsey_65plus |>   mutate(region = if_else(centroids[, "X"] < median(centroids[, "X"]), "west", "east"))  region_uncertainty <- acs_aggregate(   st_drop_geometry(ramsey_regions),   group_var = "region",   value_cols = "estimate_65plus",   moe_cols = "moe_65plus" ) |>   mutate(     cv_65plus = acs_cv(estimate_65plus, moe_65plus),     relative_moe = moe_65plus / estimate_65plus   )  region_shapes <- ramsey_regions |>   group_by(region) |>   summarize(.groups = "drop") |>   left_join(region_uncertainty, by = "region")  region_uncertainty ggplot(region_shapes) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = relative_moe), color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +   geom_sf_text(aes(label = region), color = "white", fontface = "bold") +   scale_fill_gradient(     low = "#31572c",     high = "#bc4749",     labels = scales::percent,     name = "MOE / estimate"   ) +   labs(     title = "Aggregated uncertainty for custom regions",     subtitle = "Toy west/east split, shown only as an aggregation example"   ) +   theme_void()

title: Mapping ACS uncertainty with tidycensus
text: default aggregation assumes zero covariance, matching standard Census approximation. want sensitivity analysis, cov_strategy = "constant" lets compare simple common-correlation assumption. estimate ACS covariance; propagates assumption explicitly pass. important distinction methodological: acsmoe can propagate covariance supplied, infer covariance structure ACS tabular MOEs. inference problem separate mechanics propagation.
code: zero_cov <- acs_aggregate(   st_drop_geometry(ramsey_regions),   group_var = "region",   value_cols = "estimate_65plus",   moe_cols = "moe_65plus",   cov_strategy = "zero" ) |>   mutate(strategy = "zero covariance")  positive_corr <- acs_aggregate(   st_drop_geometry(ramsey_regions),   group_var = "region",   value_cols = "estimate_65plus",   moe_cols = "moe_65plus",   cov_strategy = "constant",   cov_value = 0.20 ) |>   mutate(strategy = "constant correlation = 0.20")  bind_rows(zero_cov, positive_corr) |>   mutate(relative_moe = moe_65plus / estimate_65plus) |>   select(region, strategy, estimate_65plus, moe_65plus, relative_moe)

title: Using acsmoe with tidycensus
text: example mirrors shape Walker’s MOE vignette: pull tract-level ACS age--sex cells, aggregate cells derived total. call evaluated vignette requires network access , many setups, Census API key. covariance supplied, acs_sum() intentionally reduces zero-covariance root-sum-square calculation used tidycensus::moe_sum(). makes drop-bridge standard workflow explicit uncertainty propagation. package website includes fuller tidycensus example evaluated maps site built Census API credentials. example kept CRAN vignette evaluation requires network access, sf geometries, current ACS API availability.
code: library(tidycensus) library(dplyr) library(acsmoe)  vars <- paste0("B01001_0", c(20:25, 44:49))  ramsey <- get_acs(   geography = "tract",   variables = vars,   state = "MN",   county = "Ramsey",   year = 2016 )  ramsey65 <- ramsey |>   group_by(GEOID) |>   summarize(     estimate_65plus = sum(estimate),     moe_65plus = acs_sum(estimate, moe)$moe,     .groups = "drop"   )

title: Using acsmoe with tidycensus
text: Many ACS workflows become wide tidycensus::get_acs(output = "wide"), user-created join. acs_aggregate() handles paired-column form. default cov_strategy = "zero" deliberately conservative API sense: matches standard Census approximation behavior. read claim tract estimates truly independent.
code: library(acsmoe)  tracts <- data.frame(   region = c("north", "north", "south", "south"),   population = c(1000, 1200, 900, 1100),   population_moe = c(120, 140, 100, 130),   households = c(420, 500, 360, 440),   households_moe = c(60, 70, 50, 65) )  acs_aggregate(   tracts,   group_var = "region",   value_cols = c("population", "households"),   moe_cols = c("population_moe", "households_moe") ) #>   region population population_moe households households_moe #> 1  north       2200       184.3909        920       92.19544 #> 2  south       2000       164.0122        800       82.00610

what: Add covariance when you have it
title: Using acsmoe with tidycensus
text: covariance matrix available external method, pass standard-error scale. pass covariance MOEs. aggregation, cov_strategy = "constant" accepts scalar correlation constructs valid covariance matrix input MOEs. useful sensitivity analysis, automatic estimator ACS covariance.
code: estimates <- c(1000, 1200) moes <- c(120, 140) ses <- moe_to_se(moes)  cov_mat <- matrix(   c(ses[1]^2, 1500,     1500, ses[2]^2),   nrow = 2 )  acs_sum(estimates, moes, cov = cov_mat) #>   estimate      moe       se #> 1     2200 205.2234 124.7669 acs_aggregate(   tracts,   group_var = "region",   value_cols = "population",   moe_cols = "population_moe",   cov_strategy = "constant",   cov_value = 0.25 ) #>   region population population_moe #> 1  north       2200       205.9126 #> 2  south       2000       182.7567

what: What this package does not do
title: Using acsmoe with tidycensus
text: acsmoe download ACS data. Use tidycensus . acsmoe estimate variance microdata. Use survey srvyr PUMS replicate-weight workflows. acsmoe also implement regionalization. Walker’s tidycensus MOE article points readers Spielman Folch’s regionalization work old Python implementation. historical code lives https://github.com/geoss/censumander. used development-reference material formula checks, regionalization scope package. boundary intentional: acsmoe focuses propagation uncertainty tabular estimate/MOE workflows ACS data already obtained.

title: Using acsmoe with tidycensus
text: U.S. Census Bureau. 2020. Understanding Using American Community Survey Data: Data Users Need Know. See Chapter 8, “Calculating Measures Error Derived Estimates.” https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/library/handbooks/general.html Walker, Kyle, Matt Herman. 2025. tidycensus: Load US Census Boundary Attribute Data tidyverse sf-Ready Data Frames. https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=tidycensus Walker, Kyle. “Margins error ACS.” https://walker-data.com/tidycensus/articles/margins--error.html Spielman, Seth E., David Folch, Nicholas Nagle. 2014. “Patterns Causes Uncertainty American Community Survey.” Applied Geography 46: 147-157. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.apgeog.2013.11.002 Spielman, Seth E., David C. Folch. 2015. “Reducing Uncertainty American Community Survey Data-Driven Regionalization.” PLOS ONE 10(2): e0115626. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0115626 “Spatial Variation Quality American Community Survey Estimates.” Demography 53(5): 1535-1554. https://doi.org/10.1007/s13524-016-0499-1 Folch, David C., Seth E. Spielman. geoss/censumander. Historical Python reference implementation used development validation. https://github.com/geoss/censumander

title: Authors and Citation
text: Dmitry Shkolnik. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Shkolnik D (2026). acsmoe: Propagate Uncertainty ACS Tabular Estimates. R package version 0.1.0.9000, https://github.com/dshkol/acsmoe.
code: @Manual{,   title = {acsmoe: Propagate Uncertainty for ACS Tabular Estimates},   author = {Dmitry Shkolnik},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/dshkol/acsmoe}, }

title: ACS MOE Uncertainty Propagation
text: acsmoe early R package skeleton propagating uncertainty American Community Survey tabular workflows. scoped estimate + MOE workflows, ACS data fetching microdata variance estimation. current implementation includes: covariance-aware sums, differences, ratios, proportions, products, linear combinations; simulation helpers derived quantities; grouped geographic-style aggregation paired estimate/MOE columns; MOE/SE/CI conversion utilities; coefficient--variation reliability diagnostics; tests tidycensus formulas.

title: ACS MOE Uncertainty Propagation
text: zero-covariance formulas match U.S. Census Bureau’s ACS guidance derived estimates, especially Chapter 8 Understanding Using American Community Survey Data: Data Users Need Know. standard R workflow baseline MOE helpers provided tidycensus; see Walker Herman’s package Walker’s MOE vignette: https://walker-data.com/tidycensus/articles/margins--error.html. broader motivation ACS uncertainty handling regionalization comes Spielman, Folch, Nagle, Arribas-Bel, Koschinsky’s ACS uncertainty papers. old geoss/censumander repository cited historical reference material ; regionalization part package. Run citation("acsmoe") installation full reference details.

title: Aggregate paired ACS estimate and MOE columns by group. — acs_aggregate
text: data data frame containing ACS estimate MOE columns. group_var Grouping column, supplied string bare column name. value_cols Character vector estimate column names aggregate. moe_cols Character vector MOE column names paired value_cols. cov_strategy Covariance strategy: "zero", "supplied", "constant". cov_value "constant", scalar correlation. "supplied", named list full covariance matrices keyed estimate column. conf Confidence level associated input output MOEs.

what: acsmoe: Propagate Uncertainty for ACS Tabular Estimates — acsmoe-package
title: acsmoe: Propagate Uncertainty for ACS Tabular Estimates — acsmoe-package
text: acsmoe provides utilities American Community Survey workflows already published estimates margins error. complements tidycensus matching standard zero-covariance ACS MOE formulas default allowing users supply covariance structures available.

title: acsmoe: Propagate Uncertainty for ACS Tabular Estimates — acsmoe-package
text: package download ACS data, estimate variance microdata, implement regionalization. Use tidycensus data access survey srvyr microdata/replicate-weight workflows. Core references include U.S. Census Bureau ACS handbook guidance derived estimates, Walker Herman's tidycensus package, ACS uncertainty literature Spielman, Folch, Nagle, Arribas-Bel, Koschinsky. See README vignettes full references.

title: acsmoe: Propagate Uncertainty for ACS Tabular Estimates — acsmoe-package
text: Maintainer: Dmitry Shkolnik dmitry@dshkol.com
